title: Polymarket Seeks Approval to Bring Margin Trading to U.S. Customers
summary: Polymarket has applied to offer margin trading to U.S. customers, following a similar approval granted to Kalshi in March. This could expand trading options but also introduces higher risks for users.
body:
Polymarket, a popular decentralized prediction market platform, has submitted an application to the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) seeking approval to offer margin trading to its U.S. customers. Margin trading allows users to take positions that are not fully collateralized, effectively borrowing funds to increase potential gains—or losses. This move comes on the heels of Kalshi, a rival platform, receiving similar authorization from the CFTC in March 2026.

The application is part of Polymarket's efforts to expand its offerings and attract more traders. Margin trading can significantly amplify both profits and losses, making it a high-risk, high-reward option. The CFTC's decision will likely hinge on whether the platform can demonstrate robust risk management and consumer protection measures. If approved, Polymarket could become one of the first decentralized platforms to offer such services in the U.S.

For everyday traders, this development could mean more opportunities to leverage their positions, potentially leading to higher returns. However, it also introduces substantial risks, as margin trading can result in substantial losses if the market moves against a trader's position. Users will need to carefully consider their risk tolerance and understanding of the market before engaging in such activities.

The CFTC's decision on Polymarket's application is expected within the next few months. Traders interested in margin trading should keep an eye on the regulatory outcome and any additional guidelines that may be imposed. For now, users should continue to trade with fully collateralized positions until further notice. Read more → https://www.coindesk.com/markets/2026/07/10/polymarket-seeks-approval-to-bring-margin-trading-to-u-s-customers

If approved, U.S. traders could soon leverage positions. But beware: margin trading is high-risk. Watch for CFTC's decision → https://www.cryptocatalyst.news/articles/2026-07-10-polymarket-seeks-cftc-approval-for-margin-trading-in-the-us
standaloneTweet: Margin trading could come to U.S. users via Polymarket, but it's a double-edged sword—higher rewards come with higher risks. Stay tuned for CFTC's decision!
